question: What's the best time to visit Wollongong NSW with kids?
answer: Spring (September-November) and autumn (March-May) are absolute ripper times to visit Wollongong with the little ones! You'll get beautiful mild weather, fewer crowds, and more affordable rates at holiday parks. Summer's brilliant for beach days but gets busy during school hols. Winter's surprisingly lovely too – crisp mornings, sunny days, and you'll practically have the beaches to yourselves!

question: Are there pet friendly holiday parks in Wollongong NSW?
answer: You bet! Many holiday parks around Wollongong welcome your furry family members. Places like BIG4 Wollongong Beach and Lakeside Holiday Park have pet-friendly cabins and sites. Just remember to book ahead and specify you're bringing pets, as they usually have designated areas. Most require dogs to be leashed and well-behaved. Always check individual park policies before booking, mate!

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Wollongong NSW during school holidays?
answer: During school holidays, expect to pay $150-350 per night for a family cabin, depending on size and facilities. Powered sites for caravans run $50-90 nightly. Easter and Christmas holidays are priciest. Pro tip: book 6-12 months ahead for better rates and availability. Off-peak times can be 30-40% cheaper, so consider pulling the kids out for a cheeky midweek adventure!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Pack swimmers, towels, sunscreen (SPF50+), hats, and insect repellent – absolute essentials! Bring bikes, scooters, or outdoor toys for park fun. Most cabins have basic kitchenware, but BYO favourite snacks and breakfast bits. Don't forget a first aid kit, night lights, and comfort items for bedtime. Wet weather gear's handy year-round. Board games are great for downtime too!

question: What is there to do near Wollongong NSW holiday parks for families?
answer: Heaps! The beaches are stunning for swimming and rock pooling. Visit Nan Tien Temple, take the Seacliff Bridge walk, or explore Science Space museum. Symbio Wildlife Park's only 30 mins north – kids love it! There's the famous Kiama Blowhole nearby, plus gorgeous coastal walks. Many holiday parks have playgrounds, jumping pillows, and pools onsite, so entertainment's sorted!
